title: potentilla agrimonioides subsp. oreodoxa
intro: Potentilla agrimonioides subsp. oreodoxa is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, known for its potent medicinal properties. The plant, also known as sticky cinquefoil, has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla agrimonioides subsp. oreodoxa include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
